content.cn_text: 你将听到两名建筑学学生 Luke 和 Millie 讨论他们的作业。
content.en_text: You will hear two architecture students called Luke and Millie discussing their assignment

content.cn_text: 作业主题是塑料作为建筑材料的使用。首先，你有一些时间浏览
content.en_text: about the use of plastic as a building material. First, you have some time to look at questions

content.cn_text: 第21至第24题。
现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: Luke：嗨，Millie。 Millie：嗨，Luke。我们今天是来讨论第一项关于替代性
content.en_text: Luke: Hi, Millie. Millie: Hi, Luke. So we're here to talk over our first assignment on alternative

content.cn_text: 建筑材料的作业。 Luke：是的，我觉得进一步了解塑料如何被使用会很有意思。
content.en_text: building materials. Luke: Yeah, I think it will be really interesting to learn more about

content.cn_text: 我找到了很多20世纪60年代的资料。
content.en_text: how plastic has been used. I found lots of information from the 1960s.

content.cn_text: 那你有没有发现为什么塑料建筑这个想法在当时不受公众欢迎？
content.en_text: So did you find out why the idea of plastic buildings wasn't popular with the public

content.cn_text: Millie：不是因为材料的环保资质。
content.en_text: at that time? Millie：It wasn't the material's environmental credentials. The concept of

content.cn_text: 塑料房屋这个概念只是太未来化了。 Luke: 所以你的意思是因为它们的外观？ Millie：不是。
content.en_text: plastic houses was just too futuristic. Luke: So you mean it was their appearance? Millie：No. People

content.cn_text: 人们喜欢它们的现代设计，但把那种建筑当作家的想法就是不吸引人。
content.en_text: liked their modern design, but the idea of that sort of building as a home just didn't

content.cn_text: Luke：明白。我研究了一些工程师在20世纪60年代和70年代
content.en_text: appeal. Luke: Right. I did some research on the kind of problems

content.cn_text: 使用旧塑料时遇到的问题。 Millie：你发现了什么？
content.en_text: engineers had with the old plastics in the 1960s and 1970s. Millie：What did you learn?

content.cn_text: Luke：首先，因为塑料重量很轻，这些建筑在
content.en_text: Luke：Well firstly, because plastic doesn't weigh very much, the buildings weren't very good

content.cn_text: 大风天气中表现不太好。他们通过修改建筑形状解决了这个问题，但
content.en_text: in windy weather. They managed to fix that by amending the shape of the buildings, but

content.cn_text: 真正的缺点是，当塑料暴露在光线下时，会出现
content.en_text: the real disadvantage was that when the plastic was exposed to the light, it developed

content.cn_text: 细小裂缝，当雨水进入这些裂缝后，塑料会变形并失去
content.en_text: fine cracks, and when rain got into these cracks, the plastic would deform and lose

content.cn_text: 强度。 Millie：明白。Luke：我想知道我们在哪里能找到更多
content.en_text: its strength. Millie：Right. Luke：I wonder where we can find some more

content.cn_text: 关于当今情况的信息。 Millie：也许我们可以问一位博士生。
content.en_text: information about the situation nowadays. Millie: Maybe we could ask one of the PhD students.

content.cn_text: Luke：我想他们会太忙。 Millie：是的，没错。那我们总可以用互联网。
content.en_text: Luke: I expect they'll be too busy. Millie: Yeah, that's true. Well, we could always use the internet.

content.cn_text: Luke：也许我们应该研究更学术的来源，比如图书馆里的期刊。
content.en_text: Luke: Maybe we should research more academic sources like the journals we have in the library.

content.cn_text: Millie：好的。这样我们就能在作业最后正确引用所有内容。
content.en_text: Millie: Okay. That way we can reference everything properly at the end of the assignment.

content.cn_text: 你读过 Peterson 关于塑料房屋的文章吗？ Luke：读过，很不错。Millie：但它没有
content.en_text: Have you read Peterson's article about plastic houses? Luke: Yeah, it was really good. Millie：But it didn't

content.cn_text: 足够多房子的插图。这很可惜。它们本来可以作为展示中的例子。
content.en_text: have enough illustrations of the houses. That was a shame. They'd have been useful

content.cn_text: 不过我喜欢它的组织方式。
content.en_text: for us as examples in our presentation. I liked the way it was organised though. That

content.cn_text: 真的很清楚。 Luke：是的，而且它不太技术化，但你确实需要先了解一点
content.en_text: was really clear. Luke: Yeah, and it wasn't too technical, but you did need to know a bit

content.cn_text: 塑料房屋，才能充分理解它。 Millie：确实。
content.en_text: about plastic houses before you read it to get the most out of it. Millie: That's true.

content.cn_text: 在听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间浏览第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25-30.

content.cn_text: 现在请听录音，并回答第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25-30.

content.cn_text: Millie：好的。那我们开始比较我们的发现吧？ Luke：当然。我来记些笔记。
content.en_text: Millie: Okay. Well, shall we start comparing our findings then? Luke: Sure. I'll make some notes.

content.cn_text: Millie：我读到的最早案例是一座叫 The House of the Future 的塑料房屋，建于1957年，虽然实际上没有人住过。
content.en_text: Millie：Well, the earliest one I read about was a plastic house called The House of the Future that was built in 1957, though no one actually lived in it.

content.cn_text: 它在美国一个主题公园展出了10年。之后他们拆除了它，
content.en_text: It was on show at a theme park in America for 10 years. After that, they demolished it,

content.cn_text: 但过程很费劲。它真的非常耐用。他们无法把塑料拆碎。
content.en_text: but they had a hard time. It was really built to last. They couldn't break the plastic up.

content.cn_text: Luke：那是有超声波洗碗机的那座吗？ Millie：是的，即使今天看来也很
content.en_text: Luke: Was that the one that had an ultrasonic dishwashing machine? Millie: Yes, that would still be pretty

content.cn_text: 前沿。 Luke：你读过 The Wonderhouse 吗？它是1964年由一位工业设计师设计的，
content.en_text: revolutionary today. Luke: Did you read about The Wonderhouse? It was designed in 1964 by an industrial

content.cn_text: 叫 Alfred Noyes。 Millie：哦，就是屋顶像帐篷或雨伞那座。
content.en_text: designer called Alfred Noyes. Millie：Oh, the one with the roof like a sort of tent or an umbrella.

content.cn_text: Luke：没错。屋顶由玻璃纤维制成，在每个角落向下弯曲到地面，
content.en_text: Luke: That's right. The roof was made of fiberglass and it curved down to the ground at each corner,

content.cn_text: 围合出一个大的中央空间。然后侧面填入玻璃或塑料。这在当时是一个非常
content.en_text: enclosing a big central space. Then the sides were filled with glass or plastic. It was a really

content.cn_text: 创新的想法，但 Noyes 只做了一个小模型，这个设计没有被实施。
content.en_text: innovative idea for the time, but Noyes only made a small model and the design wasn't implemented.

content.cn_text: Millie：嗯，真可惜。Luke: 那1968到1978年间建造的 spaceship houses 呢？像飞碟一样。
content.en_text: Millie：Hmm, pity. Luke: What about those spaceship houses they built between 1968 and 1978? Like flying

content.cn_text: 他们建了一百多座。 Millie：我不知道我是否想住在里面。居住
content.en_text: saucers. They built over a hundred of them. Millie: I don't know if I'd want to live in one. The living

content.cn_text: 空间相当有限，而且天花板很低，但它们确实看起来非常未来化。
content.en_text: area was quite limited and they had really low ceilings, but they do look really futuristic,

content.cn_text: 不是吗？Luke: 白色墙壁和圆形造型。是的。不过我喜欢的塑料房是
content.en_text: don't they? Luke: With their white walls and circular shape, yes. But the plastic houses I like are the

content.cn_text: 1979到1983年间建造的泡沫房屋。它们有非常弯曲的形状。它们是通过
content.en_text: foam houses they built between 1979 and 1983. They've got really curvy shapes. They built them by

content.cn_text: 在一个巨大的气球上喷泡沫建成的。Millie：它们部分是为了展示计算机在家庭中能做什么。
content.en_text: spraying foam over a great big balloon. Millie：They were partly built to show what computers could do in

content.cn_text: 比如控制温度和灯光等。这在当时是相当新的想法。
content.en_text: the home. Things like controlling heat and light and so on. Quite new ideas at the time.

content.cn_text: 而且它们隔热性能也很好，所以能节省能源。那时人们刚开始关注这个。
content.en_text: And they were very well insulated as well, so they saved energy. People were just beginning to get

content.cn_text: Luke：我读到过一种塑料展厅，整个都可以收纳进一个相对较小的
content.en_text: into that. Luke: I read about a plastic exhibition hall that can all be packed away in a relatively small

content.cn_text: 容器里。你只要把它放到卡车上，运到想要的地方，然后充气。
content.en_text: container. You just put it on a truck and take it to wherever you want it, then inflate it.

content.cn_text: 就是把它充满空气，里面能容纳几百人。展览或
content.en_text: You know, fill it up with air and there's room for hundreds of people. And when the exhibition or

content.cn_text: 其他活动结束后，你可以把它拆下来，并一次又一次重复使用。
content.en_text: whatever's over, you just take it down again and you can reuse it again and again.

content.cn_text: Millie：还有一座新西兰的房子，是用家庭和工业废料建成的，比如塑料箱。
content.en_text: Millie: And there's a house in New Zealand that's built from household and industrial waste, like plastic boxes.

content.cn_text: 他们说从隔热角度看，这是一种比砖更好的建筑材料。
content.en_text: They say it's a much better construction material than bricks from the point of view of insulation.

content.cn_text: Luke：是的，他们也在世界其他地方用这个想法建房。
content.en_text: Luke: Yes, they're using that idea to build houses in other parts of the world too.

content.cn_text: 好的，看起来我们已经有很多材料了。
content.en_text: Okay, well it looks as if we've got plenty of stuff.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。你现在有30秒时间检查第三部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of part three. You now have 30 seconds to check your answers to part three.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21 – 24

items.title: Why did buildings made of plastic fail to please the public in the 1960s?

options.title: A
options.content: People didn't think they were good for the environment.

options.title: B
options.content: People didn't want to live in them.

options.title: C
options.content: People didn't find them visually appealing.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问为什么20世纪60年代塑料建筑没能取悦公众。原文说人们喜欢现代设计，但不喜欢把那种建筑当作 home，所以答案是 B. People didn't want to live in them。环保和外观都被否定。

items.title: In the 1960s and 1970s, engineers were unable to overcome the problem of

options.title: A
options.content: damage caused in the production process.

options.title: B
options.content: the effects of light and water.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问20世纪60和70年代工程师无法克服什么问题。原文说 wind 的问题通过改形状解决了，但真正缺点是 light 和 rain 导致裂缝、变形和失强，所以答案是 B. the effects of light and water。

items.title: Luke and Millie agree to try to get more information about present-day plastics from
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问 Luke 和 Millie 同意从哪里获取更多当今塑料信息。博士生太忙，互联网只是备选，最后他们同意使用图书馆里的 journals，所以答案是 A. journals。

items.title: What do Luke and Millie agree about the article by Peterson?

options.title: C
options.content: It was a good introduction to the topic.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问两人同意 Peterson 的文章如何。原文说文章组织 clear、not too technical，说明容易理解，所以答案是 B. It was easy to understand。但它缺少 useful examples，因此 A 不对；也不是入门介绍，因为需要先有一些背景知识。

groups.desc: Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–G, next to questions 25–30.
groups.title: Questions 25 – 30

items.title: the ‘house of the future’ (1957)
items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问 The House of the Future 对应什么描述。原文说它 built to last，拆除时很难破坏塑料，所以答案是 G. very strong construction。

items.title: the ‘wonderhouse’ (1964)
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 The Wonderhouse 对应什么描述。原文说 only made a small model and the design wasn't implemented，所以答案是 E. never actually built。

items.title: spaceship houses (1968–78)
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问 spaceship houses 对应什么描述。原文说 living area limited and low ceilings，说明内部空间不大，所以答案是 B. not much room inside。

items.title: foam houses (1979–83)
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问 foam houses 对应什么描述。原文说它们部分是为了展示 computers could do in the home，比如控制热和光，所以答案是 D. designed to promote technology。

items.title: plastic exhibition hall
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问 plastic exhibition hall 对应什么描述。原文说可收进小容器、放卡车运走、到地方再充气，所以答案是 A. easy to transport。

items.title: New Zealand house
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问 New Zealand house 对应什么描述。原文说它由 household and industrial waste 建成，比如塑料箱，所以答案是 C. made from recycled materials。
